commit.message:
Prevent distributed claim starvation on leased job release

When a distributed worker releases a leased job (for prerequisites or
other deferrals), requeue it with updated queued_at and incremented
attempts instead of preserving original queue position. This prevents
the same ineligible head-of-queue jobs from being reclaimed endlessly
and stalling progress while other eligible jobs exist.

commit.message:
fix: RPC server auto-detects TLS vs plain HTTP on same port

Peek the first byte of each connection before deciding whether to do a
TLS handshake. TLS ClientHello always starts with 0x16; plain HTTP
starts with an ASCII method byte. This eliminates 'RPC TLS handshake
failed: tls handshake eof' spam caused by clients (wallets, peer nodes)
that connect with http:// to a rpctls=1 port. Both protocols are now
served transparently on the same port.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): separate aws+jq pipe so describe-task-definition failure is caught

The original deploy steps used a pipe inside a $() substitution:

  TASK_DEF=$(aws ecs describe-task-definition ... | jq 'del(...)')

bash's set -e does NOT propagate into command substitutions, so when
aws exits non-zero (task def not found), jq receives empty stdin and
outputs 'null', the script continues, and register-task-definition
receives invalid JSON — causing the confusing 'Invalid JSON received'
error that masks the real failure.

Fix: split the aws call into its own assignment with an explicit
'|| { echo ...; exit 1; }' guard, then pipe the captured JSON through
jq in a separate step. This makes the failure surface immediately with
an actionable message pointing to the terraform apply command.

Also adds ecs:ListClusters to the CI IAM policy — it was missing and
is required by the cluster auto-discover step added earlier.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
terraform: add sync-github-secrets.sh to automate GitHub secret management

Adds a script that reads all required CI/CD values from terraform output
and pushes them to the target GitHub environment using the gh CLI — no
manual copy-pasting from terraform output to GitHub settings.

Usage:
  bash terraform/scripts/sync-github-secrets.sh --env test
  bash terraform/scripts/sync-github-secrets.sh --env prod

Also adds three individual outputs (aws_region, api_domain,
frontend_domain) to outputs.tf so the script can read each value via
'terraform output -raw' without parsing the composite
github_secrets_summary map.

Updates terraform/README.md §6 to document the script and replace the
old manual instructions.

commit.message:
feat: token-budget illuminate — corpus-proportional output

Add max_chars and auto_budget to POST /v1/memory/explore.

auto_budget:true derives char budget from corpus size:
  budget = (atom_count × avg_content_len) / compression_ratio
  default ratio: 1000, floor: 50k chars

max_chars: explicit char budget (e.g. 200_000 ≈ 50k tokens)

When a budget is active:
- max_nodes lifts to 10,000 (BFS collects freely)
- After fetch, nodes are ranked by subgraph degree (most-connected first)
- Trim to fit budget — most important nodes kept

New stats fields: chars_used, char_budget

Usage:
  {seed:{global:true}, auto_budget:true}
  → returns the corpus spine sized to ~0.1% of your total corpus

commit.message:
feat: global illuminate mode — seed from top-degree nodes

Add seed.global:true mode to POST /v1/memory/explore.
When set, uses globalTopNodes() to find the N most interconnected
atoms in the corpus (by weighted degree centrality), then runs the
same BFS from those seeds.

This reveals the corpus 'spine' — concepts that recur most
consistently across all ingested sessions — without requiring
a search query.

New fields in ExploreRequest.seed:
  global: boolean     — enable global mode
  seed_count: number  — how many top-degree seeds to use (default 10)

commit.message:
ci: switch test-reporter jobs from windows-latest to ubuntu-latest

The ci-report workflow only runs dorny/test-reporter, which downloads
test artifacts and posts check run results. This work is
platform-independent and does not require a Windows environment.

Switching to ubuntu-latest:
- Reduces CI job startup time (~1–2 min faster per PR)
- Reduces per-minute billing cost (Windows runners are ~2x more
  expensive than Linux runners)
- No functional difference: TRX files from the test jobs are just
  downloaded and parsed, not executed

commit.message:
fix: honor CancellationToken in MoveNextAsync (closes #179)

Add ThrowIfCancellationRequested() check at the start of each
MoveNextAsync call (in the active iteration path). This ensures that
when a CancellationToken passed to GetAsyncEnumerator is cancelled,
the next call to MoveNextAsync will throw OperationCanceledException.

The check is a no-op for CancellationToken.None, so the common case
of no cancellation token has no overhead beyond a single field read.

Adds 8 tests covering:
- Pre-cancelled token throws on first MoveNextAsync
- Token cancelled mid-iteration throws on next MoveNextAsync
- Normal iteration is unaffected (no token, CancellationToken.None)
- Multiple independent enumerators respect independent tokens

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
